text: Go Migration Roadmap — incremental, rollback-able at every step

label.text: PHASE 0 Foundations & test harness: openc3-go-lib, Store/Topic wrapper (db_shard parity), S3 + AnyCable clients, golden-master fixtures. Pure additive — no prod traffic
PHASE 1 Compute core (C-ext win): structure.c/binary marshalling, crc + polynomial conv, burst sync + config lexer, Packet/Structure types. Fuzz + bench vs C ext; optional FFI back into Ruby
PHASE 2 First Go MS on live bus: Interface MS (goroutines), Streams (TCP/UDP/serial/MQTT/HTTP), protocol chain, Decom MS + models, S3 logs + QuestDB. Per-target cutover; flip config to roll back
PHASE 3 Operator & REST APIs: Go operator/supervisor, cmd-tlm-api REST, config (text DSL + XTCE), script-runner REST front (execution stays Ruby). Shadow-test then flip routes endpoint-by-endpoint
Highest ROI / lowest risk; ships even if rest stalls
KEEP (never ported): Vue SPA, AnyCable, Traefik, Valkey, versitygw, QuestDB, Ruby/Python script instrumentation
RETIRE over time: Python parallel core lib (~46k LOC) — Go becomes single non-Ruby runtime (3 impls -> 2)

text: Realistic total: a 9-15 month incremental program, value delivered at the end of each phase
